top_stories.title: Google's new Gemini API Agent Skill patches the knowledge gap AI models have with their own SDKs
top_stories.summary: AI models don't know about their own updates after training. Google's new "Agent Skill" shows how a simple fix can dramatically improve coding results. The article Google's new Gemini API Agent Skill patches the knowledge gap AI models have with their own SDKs appeared first on The Decoder.
top_stories.technical_takeaway: Agent orchestration is emerging as a new software abstraction layer.
top_stories.primary_risk: Debugging complexity and cascading failures in agentic systems.
top_stories.primary_opportunity: End-to-end automation of complex cognitive workflows.

top_stories.title: Anthropic's new data shows AI skill builds over time, and that could widen the inequality gap
top_stories.summary: Anthropic's second Economic Index tracks how Claude usage is evolving across the economy. One key finding: the longer people use the AI model, the better their results get. That could widen existing inequalities. The article Anthropic's new data shows AI skill builds over time, and that could widen the inequality gap appeared first on The Deco
top_stories.technical_takeaway: Signals incremental evolution rather than a paradigm shift in current AI systems.
top_stories.primary_risk: Marginal performance gains may not justify integration effort.
top_stories.primary_opportunity: Practical improvements when layered onto existing AI workflows.

top_stories.title: MSA: Memory Sparse Attention for Efficient End-to-End Memory Model Scaling to 100M Tokens
top_stories.summary: arXiv:2603.23516v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Long-term memory is a cornerstone of human intelligence. Enabling AI to process lifetime-scale information remains a long-standing pursuit in the field. top_stories.technical_takeaway: Signals incremental evolution rather than a paradigm shift in current AI systems.
top_stories.primary_risk: Marginal performance gains may not justify integration effort.

top_stories.summary: arXiv:2603.23701v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: In Large Language Model (LLM) inference, early-exit refers to stopping computation at an intermediate layer once the prediction is sufficiently confident, thereby reducing latency and cost. top_stories.title: Object Search in Partially-Known Environments via LLM-informed Model-based Planning and Prompt Selection
top_stories.summary: arXiv:2603.23800v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We present a novel LLM-informed model-based planning framework, and a novel prompt selection method, for object search in partially-known environments.
